title: Robust Regression Head: Fit a Loss for Corrupted Sensors
strategies: predict the training-label mean (zero weights) -- reproduces the evaluator's baseline, ~0.1
ordinary least squares (plain squared/L2 loss) -- fits the signal but chases the corrupted outliers, so it generalizes worse than a robust fit

robust loss / fitting: Huber or absolute-error IRLS, Tukey biweight, trimmed least squares, RANSAC-style outlier rejection, or a quantile loss -- downweight or discard the corrupted labels to recover the clean head, tuning delta / breakdown / reweighting schedule
explicit two-stage: fit, flag high-residual points via a robust scale (MAD) threshold, drop them, and refit least squares on the clean subset
